enonce.texte: Match the following job advertisement terms with their correct definitions. This exercise will help you understand the key vocabulary used in job postings.

questions.texte: Match each term (a-e) with its correct definition (i-v).

sousQuestions.texte: Position
sousQuestions.reponse: The job title or role available.

sousQuestions.texte: Requirements
sousQuestions.reponse: The skills, qualifications, or experience needed for the job.

sousQuestions.texte: Salary
sousQuestions.reponse: The fixed amount of money paid regularly for work.

sousQuestions.texte: Apply
sousQuestions.reponse: To formally submit an application for a job.

sousQuestions.texte: Qualification
sousQuestions.reponse: A degree, diploma, or certificate showing that you have completed a course of study.

questions.texte: Fill in the blank with the correct word from the list: [position, requirements, salary, apply, qualification].
questions.reponse: To apply for this job, you must have a degree in marketing.

sousQuestions.texte: To _______ for this job, you must have a degree in marketing.
sousQuestions.reponse: apply

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ for this position include excellent communication skills.
sousQuestions.reponse: requirements

sousQuestions.texte: The annual _______ for this job is 5,000,000 CFA francs.
sousQuestions.reponse: salary

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ of Sales Manager is now open.
sousQuestions.reponse: position

intitule: Using Modals of Advice and Obligation

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the correct modal verbs: should (advice) or have to (obligation).

questions.texte: Choose the correct modal verb to complete each sentence.

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ submit your CV before the deadline. (obligation)
sousQuestions.reponse: have to

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ wear a suit to the interview. (advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: should

sousQuestions.texte: Applicants _______ have at least two years of experience. (obligation)
sousQuestions.reponse: have to

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ prepare questions to ask the interviewer. (advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: should

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ include your references in the application. (obligation)
sousQuestions.reponse: have to

questions.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using the correct modal verb as indicated in parentheses.

sousQuestions.texte: It is necessary for you to bring your ID. (have to)
sousQuestions.reponse: You have to bring your ID.

sousQuestions.texte: It's a good idea to arrive early. (should)
sousQuestions.reponse: You should arrive early.

sousQuestions.texte: It is obligatory for all candidates to take a test. (have to)
sousQuestions.reponse: All candidates have to take a test.

enonce.texte:
Read the job advertisement below carefully and answer the questions that follow.

Job Advertisement: Marketing Assistant

We are looking for a dynamic Marketing Assistant to join our team in Douala. The ideal candidate should have a degree in Marketing or a related field. You must have excellent communication skills and be proficient in Microsoft Office. Previous experience in a marketing role is an advantage. The position offers a competitive salary and opportunities for professional growth.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor's degree in Marketing or related field
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office
  • Ability to work in a team

To apply, please submit your CV and a cover letter to hr@company.com before June 30th.

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the job advertisement.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the job position being advertised?
sousQuestions.reponse: Marketing Assistant.

sousQuestions.texte: Where is the job located?
sousQuestions.reponse: Douala.

sousQuestions.texte: List three requirements mentioned in the advertisement.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Bachelor's degree in Marketing or related field 2. Excellent communication skills 3. Proficiency in Microsoft Office.

sousQuestions.texte: What documents must candidates submit?
sousQuestions.reponse: A CV and a cover letter.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the deadline for applications?
sousQuestions.reponse: June 30th.

enonce.texte:
Based on the job advertisement above, write a structured cover letter applying for the position of Marketing Assistant. Your letter should include the following elements:

  • Your contact information
  • The date
  • The recipient's address
  • A subject line
  • An introduction stating the position you are applying for
  • A paragraph about your qualifications and why you are suitable for the job
  • A paragraph about your experience and skills
  • A closing paragraph expressing your interest in an interview
  • A formal closing

questions.texte: Write your cover letter in the space provided. Use formal language and follow the structure given.
questions.reponse: A well-structured formal cover letter that includes all required elements.

intitule: Giving Advice for a Job Application

enonce.texte: Your friend is applying for a job. Write an email to give them advice on how to succeed in their application. Use the modal verbs should and have to appropriately.

questions.texte: Write a formal email of about 150 words giving advice to your friend. Include at least three pieces of advice using 'should' and three obligations using 'have to'.
questions.reponse: A well-structured email that correctly uses modals of advice and obligation to provide helpful guidance.

enonce.texte:
You have received two job offers. Study the information below and write a comparative analysis of the two positions. Make recommendations about which job to accept and justify your choice.

Job Offer A: Marketing Manager

  • Salary: 800,000 CFA/month
  • Location: Yaoundé
  • Benefits: Health insurance, paid vacation
  • Requirements: 5 years experience, Master's degree
  • Working hours: 8am-5pm, Monday-Friday

Job Offer B: Marketing Executive

  • Salary: 600,000 CFA/month
  • Location: Douala
  • Benefits: Transportation allowance
  • Requirements: 2 years experience, Bachelor's degree
  • Working hours: 9am-6pm, Monday-Saturday

questions.texte: Analyze the two job offers and write a comparative analysis. Use the following structure:

sousQuestions.texte: Introduction: present the two job offers
sousQuestions.reponse: The analysis begins with a clear introduction of the two positions.

sousQuestions.texte: Body: compare salary, benefits, location, requirements, and working hours
sousQuestions.reponse: A detailed comparison of all aspects of the two jobs.

sousQuestions.texte: Conclusion: recommend which job to accept and justify your choice using modals of advice and obligation
sousQuestions.reponse: A clear recommendation with justification using modal verbs.

enonce.texte: Create a complete job advertisement for a position of your choice in Cameroon. Your advertisement should be structured and clear.

questions.texte: Write a job advertisement that includes the following elements:

sousQuestions.texte: Job title and company name
sousQuestions.reponse: A specific job title and a realistic company name.

sousQuestions.texte: Job description and responsibilities
sousQuestions.reponse: At least three clear responsibilities for the position.

sousQuestions.texte: Requirements (qualifications and skills)
sousQuestions.reponse: At least four requirements using 'should' and 'have to'.

sousQuestions.texte: Salary and benefits
sousQuestions.reponse: Realistic salary range and benefits for the Cameroonian context.

sousQuestions.texte: Application instructions and deadline
sousQuestions.reponse: Clear instructions on how to apply and a specific deadline.
